title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Dr. Calgary asserts that Jacques (identified in news reports as Jack Agar) did not kill Mrs. Aigle. He explains that he was the driver of the car that picked up a hitchhiker on the night of the murder. 

Recounting his movements on November 9th, Calgary states that after visiting an old nanny in Pergas, he drove toward Redmyn to visit Canon Pisemash. At approximately 7:05 p.m., while on the main road from Redmyn to Draymour, he picked up a charming young man and drove him to the center of Dulmace. Because the police established that the crime occurred between 7:00 and 7:30 p.m., Calgary notes that his presence as the driver provides a definitive alibi for the young man.

Calgary explains that he failed to come forward sooner due to a concussion. After dropping off the passenger, he was struck by a speeding truck while attempting to buy cigarettes. The accident caused memory loss; he could not remember the accident or his subsequent trip to London until he regained consciousness in the hospital. He then traveled to Australia and Antarctica, unaware of the murder investigation.

A month prior to his current meeting, after returning to England, Calgary recognized the young man's face in old newspapers used for wrapping specimens. A conversation about eels triggered his recovered memories of that night. Upon discovering that Jack Agar had been convicted and had died of pneumonia in prison, Calgary presented the truth to the prosecutor through the defense lawyer, Mr. Marshall, in an attempt to seek justice for the deceased man's memory.
